generatedNote: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for Daraja result/error-code meanings. Canonical file: supabase/functions/_shared/daraja/daraja-error-codes.json. Copies in web/lib/ and paylod-mcp/src/ are GENERATED by scripts/sync-daraja-catalog.mjs — do not hand-edit them. `retryable` means SAFE TO CHARGE AGAIN (no risk of double-charging), NOT merely 'the user could try again'. An in-flight/pending payment is therefore NEVER retryable. Categories: customer | balance | limit | credentials | network | mpesa_system | pending | success.

codes.title: Success
codes.cause: The STK Push transaction completed and the customer's payment was received successfully.
codes.fix: Treat the transaction as paid, read the receipt from CallbackMetadata, and fulfil the order.
codes.customerMessage: Payment received — thank you!

codes.title: Insufficient M-Pesa balance
codes.cause: The customer does not have enough M-Pesa balance (including any available Fuliza overdraft) to cover the amount.
codes.fix: Nothing to fix on your side — ask the customer to top up their M-Pesa and try the payment again.
codes.customerMessage: Your M-Pesa balance is too low. Please top up and try again.

codes.title: M-Pesa system internal error
codes.cause: Safaricom's system (or the receiving shortcode / Party B) was temporarily unable to process the transaction — a transient M-Pesa-side error, not a problem with your request.
codes.fix: This is transient, but it is NOT proven that no charge was raised. Confirm the payment's final state with GET /status/:id (or the webhook) before charging again; only start a new attempt with a NEW idempotency key once you have confirmed nothing moved.
codes.customerMessage: M-Pesa had a problem and we cannot confirm the outcome. Do not pay again yet. Check your M-Pesa messages first.
sources: provenance (2026-07-18, codex security review): retryable changed true->false — 'safe to charge again' was set on non-authoritative community evidence; until no-debit is proven, false is the safe money call.

codes.title: M-Pesa system busy
codes.cause: M-Pesa is under high load and rejected the request because the system was busy. This is an M-Pesa-side condition, not your configuration.
codes.fix: Back off, then confirm the payment's final state with GET /status/:id (or the webhook) before charging again — a busy-system rejection is not proof no charge was raised. Only start a new attempt with a NEW idempotency key once you have confirmed nothing moved.
codes.customerMessage: M-Pesa is busy and we cannot confirm the outcome. Do not pay again yet. Check your M-Pesa messages first.
sources: provenance (2026-07-18, codex security review): retryable changed true->false — 'safe to charge again' was set on non-authoritative community evidence; until no-debit is proven, false is the safe money call.

codes.title: A transaction is already in process for this number
codes.cause: M-Pesa could not lock the subscriber because the customer's line already has an active USSD/M-Pesa session or an in-flight transaction, so a new STK push cannot start.
codes.fix: Ask the customer to finish or dismiss any open M-Pesa/USSD prompt, then wait 1-3 minutes. Do NOT blind-retry: the in-flight transaction may be your own earlier push, and charging again could double-charge. Confirm the outcome of the existing payment first.
codes.customerMessage: You have another M-Pesa request open. Finish or dismiss it first. Do not start a new payment until the open one clears.

codes.title: Transaction expired
codes.cause: The transaction exceeded its allowed processing window and M-Pesa expired it. Daraja says only 'Transaction expired.' and does not say why, so we do not guess — the usual reason is the customer taking too long to act on the prompt. Terminal: no money moved.
codes.fix: Let the customer re-initiate the payment and prompt them to approve promptly.
codes.customerMessage: The request expired. Please try the payment again and approve quickly.

codes.title: Error sending the STK prompt
codes.cause: M-Pesa could not send the STK prompt — usually a transient system error, or the request message (TransactionDesc) exceeded the 182-character limit.
codes.fix: Keep TransactionDesc within 182 characters. Before charging again, confirm the payment's final state with GET /status/:id (or the webhook) — a send failure is not proof no charge was raised. Only start a new attempt with a NEW idempotency key once you have confirmed nothing moved.
codes.customerMessage: We could not reach M-Pesa and we cannot confirm the outcome. Do not pay again yet. Check your M-Pesa messages first.
sources: provenance (2026-07-18, codex security review): retryable changed true->false — 'safe to charge again' was set on non-authoritative community evidence; until no-debit is proven, false is the safe money call.

codes.title: Error sending the STK prompt
codes.cause: The push could not be delivered — commonly a transient M-Pesa error, or the request message exceeds the 182-character limit.
codes.fix: Shorten the request fields (especially TransactionDesc) to stay within 182 characters. Before charging again, confirm the payment's final state with GET /status/:id (or the webhook) — a delivery failure is not proof no charge was raised. Only start a new attempt with a NEW idempotency key once you have confirmed nothing moved.
codes.customerMessage: We could not reach M-Pesa and we cannot confirm the outcome. Do not pay again yet. Check your M-Pesa messages first.
sources: provenance (2026-07-18, codex security review): retryable changed true->false — 'safe to charge again' was set on non-authoritative community evidence; until no-debit is proven, false is the safe money call.

codes.title: Payment cancelled by the customer
codes.cause: The customer received the STK prompt on their phone but pressed Cancel instead of entering their M-Pesa PIN. No money moved.
codes.fix: Nothing is wrong with your setup — offer a clear retry button so the customer can try again.
codes.customerMessage: Payment cancelled — you can try again whenever you're ready.

codes.title: The M-Pesa prompt went unanswered
codes.cause: M-Pesa sent the STK prompt but received no answer before it expired (~60s). Daraja reports this as 'DS timeout user cannot be reached', and has been OBSERVED live returning ResultDesc 'No response from user.' — the code covers BOTH the customer simply not acting on the prompt (the common case: they ignored, missed or dismissed it) AND a genuinely unreachable handset (phone off, out of coverage). Daraja does not tell us which, so neither do we. Terminal: no money moved.
codes.fix: Nothing is wrong with your setup. Offer a retry and ask the customer to keep their phone to hand and enter their PIN when the prompt appears. Only if the SAME number fails this way repeatedly is it worth checking that the line is a reachable Safaricom number.
codes.customerMessage: The M-Pesa prompt expired before it was answered. Check your phone is on, then try again and enter your PIN when it appears.
sources: observed-live: ResultCode 1037 + ResultDesc 'No response from user.' on a real STK push the customer deliberately ignored (2026-07-14)

codes.title: Wrong M-Pesa PIN
codes.cause: The customer entered the wrong M-Pesa PIN when approving the STK prompt. This is a customer input problem, NOT an issue with your credentials or configuration. NOTE Daraja words this confusingly: on an STK callback it sends ResultDesc 'The initiator information is invalid.' — that wording is about the CUSTOMER's PIN, not your initiator credentials. (The same numeric code on a B2C/C2B result genuinely IS an initiator-credential failure; that is a separate entry in this table, disambiguated by family.)
codes.fix: Nothing to change on your side — ask the customer to retry and enter their correct M-Pesa PIN.
codes.customerMessage: That M-Pesa PIN was incorrect. Please try again and enter the right PIN.

codes.title: Payment amount exceeds the M-Pesa limit
codes.cause: The requested amount is above the customer's allowed M-Pesa transaction or wallet limit (per-transaction or daily maximum).
codes.fix: Ask the customer to pay a smaller amount, or split the payment. Confirm the amount is within Safaricom's transaction limits.
codes.customerMessage: This amount is over your M-Pesa limit. Try a smaller amount.

codes.title: Buy Goods till sent as a Paybill request (or vice versa)
codes.cause: The shortcode is a Buy Goods till but the STK request used CustomerPayBillOnline (or a Paybill was sent as CustomerBuyGoodsOnline). Daraja rejects the transaction-type / shortcode mismatch.
codes.fix: Match the transaction type to the shortcode: CustomerBuyGoodsOnline + till number for a till, CustomerPayBillOnline + paybill for a paybill. Fix it in Credentials and retry.
codes.customerMessage: We hit a setup error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Still waiting for the customer's PIN
codes.cause: The STK prompt is live on the customer's phone and they have not entered their M-Pesa PIN yet. This is NOT a failure — the payment is still in flight and can still succeed. Retrying now would push a SECOND prompt and can double-charge the customer.
codes.fix: Keep polling GET /status/:id (or wait for the webhook). Do NOT retry the charge and do NOT tell the customer it failed — this payment is still live and can still succeed.
codes.customerMessage: Check your phone and enter your M-Pesa PIN to complete this payment.

codes.title: Transaction is still being processed
codes.cause: M-Pesa is still processing this STK Push — the customer may not have entered their PIN yet. This is NOT a failure. (Daraja overloads this code: on a SYNCHRONOUS API call it can instead mean 'merchant does not exist' / 'wrong credentials' — that terminal case is matched on the message, see the api_error entry for the same code.)
codes.fix: Keep polling GET /status/:id (or wait for the webhook). Do NOT retry the charge and do NOT tell the customer it failed — this payment is still live and can still succeed.
codes.customerMessage: Check your phone and enter your M-Pesa PIN to complete this payment.

codes.title: Bad Request — invalid amount or shortcode
codes.cause: The synchronous request was rejected as malformed, commonly due to an invalid amount, an invalid BusinessShortCode, or the payload being sent as form data instead of JSON.
codes.fix: Send the body as JSON, and verify the amount (a positive whole-number KES) and BusinessShortCode are valid before sending.
codes.customerMessage: We couldn't start the payment due to a setup error. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid request payload
codes.cause: The request payload is structurally invalid or missing required fields, so Daraja returns HTTP 400 before processing.
codes.fix: Validate the JSON schema and required parameters against the endpoint spec before sending.
codes.customerMessage: We couldn't start the payment due to a setup error. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid phone number
codes.cause: The PhoneNumber/PartyA value is not a valid Safaricom MSISDN in the required 2547XXXXXXXX international format.
codes.fix: Normalize the phone number to the 12-digit 2547XXXXXXXX format before sending.
codes.customerMessage: That phone number looks invalid. Please check it and try again.

codes.title: Unauthorized — invalid or expired access token
codes.cause: The OAuth access token is missing, malformed, or expired (tokens have a ~1 hour TTL), returning HTTP 401.
codes.fix: Request a fresh token and send it as an 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header, refreshing before expiry.
codes.customerMessage: We hit an authentication error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid access token
codes.cause: Daraja reports the access token as invalid on HTTP 404; often the token is fine but the shortcode is not authorized/whitelisted for that specific API.
codes.fix: Confirm the token is valid and current, and contact Safaricom to enable/whitelist the API on your production shortcode.
codes.customerMessage: We hit an authentication error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid authentication header
codes.cause: The Authorization header is missing or incorrectly formatted, so the request cannot be authenticated (HTTP 404).
codes.fix: Ensure the header is exactly 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' with no extra whitespace.
codes.customerMessage: We hit an authentication error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid initiator information
codes.cause: For secured APIs (B2C, Reversal, Transaction Status, Account Balance), the initiator username or the RSA-encrypted SecurityCredential is wrong, does not belong to the shortcode, or has expired.
codes.fix: Re-encrypt the initiator password with Safaricom's current RSA public certificate, confirm the initiator name belongs to the shortcode, and re-save it in Credentials.
codes.customerMessage: We hit a setup error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Server error — merchant does not exist or insufficient funds
codes.cause: A server-side error (HTTP 500), commonly a nonexistent merchant/shortcode or, for B2C, insufficient funds in the business account.
codes.fix: Verify the shortcode/merchant configuration and business account balance, then retry or contact Safaricom support.
codes.customerMessage: M-Pesa returned an error and we cannot confirm the outcome. Do not pay again yet. Check your M-Pesa messages first.

codes.title: Service temporarily unavailable / under maintenance
codes.cause: The Daraja service is temporarily unavailable, throttled (spike arrest), or under maintenance, returning HTTP 500.
codes.fix: Implement exponential backoff and retry later; if persistent, check the Safaricom status page or support.
codes.customerMessage: M-Pesa is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Success / Accepted
codes.cause: The C2B/B2C/Reversal/Transaction Status/Account Balance request was processed successfully (for C2B validation this is the 'Accept' response).
codes.fix: Return ResultCode 0 to accept, and process the result/receipt details from the confirmation callback.
codes.customerMessage: Payment received — thank you!

codes.title: Invalid initiator information
codes.cause: For B2C and other secured APIs, the initiator username / RSA-encrypted SecurityCredential does not match the shortcode or has expired. (Note: on an STK Push callback, ResultCode 2001 means the customer entered the wrong PIN — see the STK result entry.)
codes.fix: Re-encrypt the initiator password with Safaricom's RSA public cert, confirm the initiator belongs to the shortcode, and renew before expiry.
codes.customerMessage: We hit a setup error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Invalid MSISDN (Reject)
codes.cause: During C2B validation the payer's phone number (MSISDN) is considered invalid by your validation logic.
codes.fix: Return this reject code when the MSISDN is unrecognized, or accept if you do not need to validate the payer number.
codes.customerMessage: We couldn't validate your phone number for this payment.

codes.title: Invalid Account Number (Reject)
codes.cause: During C2B validation the account number / bill reference supplied by the payer does not exist in your system.
codes.fix: Return this reject code for unknown account references, or map/create the account before accepting.
codes.customerMessage: That account or reference number wasn't found. Please check it and try again.

codes.title: Invalid Amount (Reject)
codes.cause: During C2B validation the paid amount fails your rules (for example the wrong amount for the invoice).
codes.fix: Return this reject code when the amount is invalid, or relax the amount check if partial payments are allowed.
codes.customerMessage: That payment amount wasn't accepted. Please check the amount and try again.

codes.title: Invalid KYC Details (Reject)
codes.cause: During C2B validation the payer's KYC details do not meet your requirements.
codes.fix: Return this reject code when KYC checks fail, otherwise accept the transaction.
codes.customerMessage: We couldn't verify your details for this payment.

codes.title: Invalid Shortcode (Reject)
codes.cause: During C2B validation the shortcode targeted by the payment is considered invalid.
codes.fix: Return this reject code for an unexpected shortcode, or verify your shortcode configuration.
codes.customerMessage: We hit a setup error on our side. Please try again shortly.

codes.title: Other Error (Reject)
codes.cause: A catch-all C2B validation rejection for any other reason not covered by the specific reject codes.
codes.fix: Return this reject code for unclassified validation failures, and log the reason for follow-up.
codes.customerMessage: We couldn't accept this payment. Please try again.
